Dashboard Archive: 2026-03-05 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
# Market Momentum Analysis - Today's Data The momentum-ranker dashboard shows FSLY leading the pack with a composite score of 96.2, driven by exceptional consistency across timeframes: 1-week returns of 16.43% jumping to 1-month at 137.88% and ultimately 216.82% year-to-date, while maintaining a perfect 4-of-5 ratio passes and crossing all four major moving averages. ANRO ranks second with extreme long-term outperformance at 538.26% year-to-date and a stellar 5-of-5 ratio passes, though its near-zero bad_spy_score (0.6752 vs FSLY's 14.32) suggests it has shown minimal resilience during market downturns compared to FSLY's more balanced 99.92 spy-day score. Both tickers pass all SMA gates and show positive vs. SPY outperformance over multiple timeframes, but FSLY demonstrates more stable risk-adjusted momentum with smoother return acceleration across periods, while ANRO's outsized 6-month return (449.86%) relative to 3-month (55.08%) signals a recent acceleration that may warrant closer observation for sustainability. The correlation profiles diverge notably: FSLY correlates positively with bonds (TLT at 0.
